name: The Fool
upright: New beginnings, spontaneous actions, embracing opportunities without fear, curiosity, and a carefree approach. Encourages taking leaps of faith and trusting your instincts.
reversed: Recklessness, poor judgment, naivety, hesitation, or ignoring potential risks. May indicate fear of change or repeating past mistakes.

name: The Magician
upright: Manifesting goals through resourcefulness and skill, using available tools effectively, harnessing creativity and focused action to achieve desired outcomes.
reversed: Manipulation, deceit, unfulfilled potential, lack of planning, or feeling powerless. May suggest misuse of talents or wasted energy.

name: The High Priestess
upright: Intuition, inner wisdom, accessing subconscious knowledge, patience, and observing before acting. Trust your instincts and seek hidden truths.
reversed: Secrets, hidden agendas, ignoring intuition, superficiality, or withdrawal. Indicates confusion or misinterpretation of information.

name: The Empress
upright: Nurturing, abundance, fertility, creativity, comfort, and connection with nature. Encourages growth, care, and self-love.
reversed: Creative block, dependence on others, smothering tendencies, or neglect of self-care.

name: The Emperor
upright: Authority, structure, stability, discipline, leadership, and protection. Encourages building foundations and taking control responsibly.
reversed: Domination, rigidity, excessive control, inflexibility, or misuse of power.

name: The Hierophant
upright: Tradition, spiritual wisdom, guidance, conformity, ethics, and structured learning. Encourages seeking mentorship or following tried-and-true paths.
reversed: Challenging authority, breaking tradition, unconventional beliefs, or resisting guidance.

name: The Lovers
upright: Love, harmony, partnerships, choices, alignment of values, and emotional connection. Encourages conscious relationship decisions.
reversed: Disharmony, imbalance, misalignment of values, temptation, or strained relationships.

name: The Chariot
upright: Willpower, determination, focus, victory, and overcoming obstacles. Encourages disciplined action and perseverance.
reversed: Lack of control, scattered energy, opposition, delays, or feeling stuck.

name: Strength
upright: Courage, inner strength, patience, compassion, self-confidence, and gentle persuasion. Encourages resilience through calm determination.
reversed: Self-doubt, weakness, insecurity, or inability to control impulses.

name: The Hermit
upright: Introspection, solitude, soul-searching, guidance, and wisdom. Encourages reflection and finding inner answers.
reversed: Isolation, loneliness, withdrawal, or fear of being alone. May indicate avoiding self-reflection.

name: Wheel of Fortune
upright: Cycles, destiny, change, luck, opportunity, and turning points. Encourages adaptability and accepting life’s flow.
reversed: Resistance to change, misfortune, setbacks, or unexpected obstacles.

name: Justice
upright: Fairness, accountability, truth, law, and balance. Encourages honest decisions and ethical actions.
reversed: Dishonesty, lack of accountability, injustice, or biased outcomes.

name: The Hanged Man
upright: Surrender, letting go, new perspective, patience, and reflection. Encourages pausing and seeing things differently.
reversed: Delays, indecision, resistance, martyrdom, or inability to release control.

name: Death
upright: Transformation, endings, renewal, change, and letting go of the old. Encourages embracing transitions for growth.
reversed: Resistance to change, stagnation, fear of letting go, or delayed transformation.

name: Temperance
upright: Balance, moderation, harmony, patience, and integration. Encourages blending opposites and finding middle ground.
reversed: Imbalance, excess, discord, overindulgence, or lack of self-control.

name: The Devil
upright: Attachment, addiction, materialism, temptation, and unhealthy patterns. Encourages awareness of limitations and liberation from chains.
reversed: Breaking free from addiction, release of limiting beliefs, reclaiming power, or self-awareness of shadow tendencies.

name: The Tower
upright: Sudden change, upheaval, revelation, chaos, and awakening. Encourages release of false structures and embracing truth.
reversed: Avoidance of disaster, fear of change, delayed consequences, or clinging to instability.

name: The Star
upright: Hope, inspiration, healing, guidance, faith, and renewal. Encourages optimism and spiritual clarity.
reversed: Loss of faith, despair, disconnection, pessimism, or lack of trust in the process.

name: The Moon
upright: Illusion, intuition, dreams, subconscious, uncertainty, and hidden truths. Encourages exploring emotions and inner guidance.
reversed: Deception, fear, confusion, misinterpretation, or clarity emerging from illusions.

name: The Sun
upright: Positivity, success, vitality, joy, clarity, and confidence. Encourages embracing happiness and accomplishment.
reversed: Temporary setbacks, overconfidence, inner child issues, or misplaced optimism.

name: Judgement
upright: Rebirth, awakening, self-evaluation, accountability, and higher calling. Encourages embracing transformation and learning from past actions.
reversed: Self-doubt, denial, ignoring inner calling, fear of change, or harsh self-criticism.

name: The World
upright: Completion, accomplishment, integration, fulfillment, and harmony. Encourages celebrating achievements and global awareness.
reversed: Delays, unfinished business, lack of closure, or overambition preventing fulfillment.

name: Ace of Cups
upright: Emotional renewal, new love, compassion, spiritual awakening, and creative inspiration.
reversed: Emotional blockages, heartbreak, or inability to express feelings clearly.

name: Two of Cups
upright: Mutual attraction, partnership, harmony, reconciliation, and emotional balance.
reversed: Conflict, imbalance, breakups, or one-sided relationships.

name: Three of Cups
upright: Friendship, community, celebration, support, and collaboration.
reversed: Overindulgence, gossip, isolation from friends, or strained relationships.

name: Four of Cups
upright: Contemplation, reevaluation, introspection, boredom, or missed opportunities.
reversed: Openness to new possibilities, awareness, overcoming apathy, or emotional reconnection.

name: Five of Cups
upright: Loss, grief, disappointment, regret, and focusing on the negative.
reversed: Acceptance, moving forward, healing, or seeing opportunities amidst loss.

name: Six of Cups
upright: Nostalgia, childhood memories, innocence, kindness, and past connections.
reversed: Living in the past, unrealistic expectations, or difficulty letting go.

name: Seven of Cups
upright: Choices, imagination, dreams, possibilities, and wishful thinking.
reversed: Illusions, confusion, poor decisions, or difficulty committing.

name: Eight of Cups
upright: Walking away, seeking deeper meaning, leaving behind what no longer serves you.
reversed: Fear of change, avoidance, stagnation, or returning to past situations.

name: Nine of Cups
upright: Emotional fulfillment, satisfaction, happiness, and wishes coming true.
reversed: Greed, dissatisfaction, unrealistic expectations, or overindulgence.

name: Ten of Cups
upright: Happiness, family harmony, lasting relationships, and emotional contentment.
reversed: Family disputes, broken relationships, or disconnection from emotional fulfillment.

name: Ace of Pentacles
upright: New financial opportunities, prosperity, manifestation, and material abundance.
reversed: Missed opportunities, financial setbacks, lack of planning, or greed.

name: Two of Pentacles
upright: Balance, adaptability, time management, multitasking, and flexibility.
reversed: Overwhelm, disorganization, financial instability, or poor prioritization.

name: Three of Pentacles
upright: Teamwork, collaboration, skill recognition, and successful projects.
reversed: Lack of teamwork, conflicts, undervaluation of skills, or poor collaboration.

name: Four of Pentacles
upright: Financial security, stability, saving, control, and holding on to resources.
reversed: Greed, possessiveness, financial insecurity, or inability to share.

name: Five of Pentacles
upright: Financial loss, poverty, hardship, insecurity, and feeling left out.
reversed: Recovery, hope, assistance, overcoming struggles, or support from others.

name: Six of Pentacles
upright: Generosity, charity, balance between giving and receiving, financial fairness.
reversed: Debt, selfishness, imbalance, or exploitation.

name: Seven of Pentacles
upright: Patience, investment, assessment, long-term planning, and reward for effort.
reversed: Impatience, lack of progress, wasted effort, or poor planning.

name: Eight of Pentacles
upright: Skill development, hard work, dedication, mastery, and learning.
reversed: Laziness, lack of focus, poor craftsmanship, or ignoring growth opportunities.

name: Nine of Pentacles
upright: Self-sufficiency, luxury, financial independence, and success earned through effort.
reversed: Over-dependence, financial setbacks, isolation, or superficial achievements.

name: Ten of Pentacles
upright: Wealth, family legacy, stability, long-term success, and security.
reversed: Financial loss, family conflicts, instability, or short-term thinking.

name: Ace of Swords
upright: Clarity, new ideas, mental breakthroughs, truth, and decisive action.
reversed: Confusion, misinformation, delays, miscommunication, or misuse of intellect.

name: Two of Swords
upright: Difficult decisions, stalemate, weighing options, and finding balance.
reversed: Indecision, avoidance, confusion, or forced choices.

name: Three of Swords
upright: Heartbreak, sorrow, betrayal, emotional pain, and grief.
reversed: Healing, forgiveness, recovery, or lingering emotional pain.

name: Four of Swords
upright: Rest, recuperation, contemplation, meditation, and recovery from stress.
reversed: Restlessness, burnout, refusal to rest, or delayed recovery.

name: Five of Swords
upright: Conflict, tension, competition, dishonor, or ethical dilemmas.
reversed: Resolution, reconciliation, walking away, or learning from mistakes.

name: Six of Swords
upright: Transition, moving on, leaving difficulties behind, healing, and finding peace after struggle.
reversed: Resistance to change, emotional baggage, or feeling stuck in turbulent times.

name: Seven of Swords
upright: Deception, strategy, cleverness, stealth, and acting independently. Encourages tactful approaches and caution.
reversed: Confession, exposure, guilt, or being caught in deceit. Encourages honesty and reevaluation of motives.

name: Eight of Swords
upright: Restriction, fear, self-imposed limitations, or feeling trapped by circumstances.
reversed: Liberation, empowerment, overcoming fear, or new perspectives that free the mind.

name: Nine of Swords
upright: Anxiety, nightmares, guilt, mental anguish, and overthinking. Represents fears taking control of the mind.
reversed: Healing, release of worry, acceptance, or confronting fears with clarity.

name: Ten of Swords
upright: Betrayal, painful endings, loss, crisis, and rock bottom — but also the potential for rebirth.
reversed: Recovery, regeneration, surviving hardship, and learning from past pain.

name: Page of Swords
upright: Curiosity, intellect, vigilance, communication, and thirst for knowledge. A message of insight or clarity.
reversed: Gossip, deception, impulsiveness, or hasty speech leading to misunderstandings.

name: Knight of Swords
upright: Ambition, action, assertiveness, and determination. Encourages swift progress and direct communication.
reversed: Recklessness, impatience, aggression, or acting without considering consequences.

name: Queen of Swords
upright: Intellect, independence, perception, fairness, and clear communication. Represents truth and discernment.
reversed: Coldness, bitterness, manipulation, or overly harsh judgment.

name: King of Swords
upright: Authority, logic, reason, integrity, and wise decision-making. Represents mental clarity and leadership.
reversed: Tyranny, misuse of power, manipulation, or unethical behavior.

name: Ace of Wands
upright: Inspiration, new beginnings, passion, creativity, and growth. A spark of motivation or opportunity.
reversed: Delays, lack of direction, creative blocks, or lost enthusiasm.

name: Two of Wands
upright: Planning, foresight, personal power, and making long-term choices. The courage to look ahead.
reversed: Fear of the unknown, indecision, poor planning, or missed opportunities.

name: Three of Wands
upright: Expansion, foresight, success through vision, and progress in ventures.
reversed: Delays, obstacles, lack of foresight, or frustration in progress.

name: Four of Wands
upright: Celebration, harmony, homecoming, stability, and joyful milestones.
reversed: Tension at home, canceled celebrations, or temporary instability.

name: Five of Wands
upright: Conflict, competition, tension, and differing opinions. Can indicate spirited debate or challenge.
reversed: Resolution, cooperation, end of conflict, or avoiding unnecessary drama.

name: Six of Wands
upright: Victory, recognition, success, public acclaim, and progress after struggle.
reversed: Failure, ego, lack of recognition, or misplaced pride.

name: Seven of Wands
upright: Perseverance, defense, standing your ground, and courage against opposition.
reversed: Giving up, exhaustion, defensiveness, or feeling overwhelmed.

name: Eight of Wands
upright: Speed, communication, swift progress, and decisive movement. Momentum is building toward success.
reversed: Delays, miscommunication, chaos, or frustration in plans.

name: Nine of Wands
upright: Resilience, persistence, endurance, and protecting what you’ve built. Courage despite fatigue.
reversed: Paranoia, stubbornness, burnout, or refusing to compromise.

name: Ten of Wands
upright: Burden, responsibility, hard work, and feeling weighed down by obligations.
reversed: Release, delegation, avoidance, or lightening your load for relief.

name: Page of Wands
upright: Enthusiasm, discovery, creative exploration, and messages of opportunity.
reversed: Restlessness, impulsiveness, lack of direction, or missed inspiration.

name: Knight of Wands
upright: Adventure, passion, confidence, and pursuit of dreams with energy and boldness.
reversed: Impulsiveness, arrogance, burnout, or scattered energy without focus.

name: Queen of Wands
upright: Charisma, creativity, determination, and self-assurance. Represents bold action and charm.
reversed: Jealousy, insecurity, selfishness, or manipulation through charm.

name: King of Wands
upright: Leadership, vision, courage, and entrepreneurship. Represents mastery of creative energy and strategy.
reversed: Impulsiveness, domineering attitude, arrogance, or lack of direction.
